content.problemStatement:
One day, Chef prepared **D** brand new dishes. He named the i-th dish by a string **Si**. After the cooking, he decided to categorize each of these **D** dishes as special or not.



A dish **Si** is called special if its name (i.e. the string **Si**) can be represented in the form of a double string by removing at most one (possibly zero) character from it's name from any position. 



A string is called a double string if it can be represented as a concatenation of two identical, non-empty strings. 
e.g. "abab" is a double string as it can be represented as "ab" + "ab" where + operation denotes concatenation. 
Similarly, "aa", "abcabc" are double strings whereas "a", "abba", "abc" are not.
constraints: Sum of lengths of all Si does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains D, the number of dishes. Each of the next D lines contains a non-empty string Si consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each dish, output a line containing 'YES' if the dish name can become a double string after removing at most one character, otherwise output 'NO'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: aba (length 3) -> delete the middle 'b' to obtain "aa", which is a double string. abac (length 4) is even but halves "ab" and "ac" differ, and removing any one character makes length odd, so NO. abcd cannot be turned into a double string with one deletion.
abcabc is already a double string ("abc"+"abc"). abc (length 3) after any single deletion becomes length 2, but the two remaining characters are never identical, so NO.
abab is already a double string ("ab"+"ab"). No deletion is needed.

solution.approach: The key insight is to check if a string can be represented as a double string by removing at most one character. This can be done by checking if the string is already a double string, or if removing one character from the left or right side makes it a double string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(D*N) total, where N is the maximum length of a string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the string itself.

code.JavaScript: const lines=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split('\n');let idx=0;const D=parseInt(lines[idx++],10);let out=[];function isDouble(s){const half=s.length>>1;return s.slice(0,half)===s.slice(half);}function canDeleteFromLeft(s){const n=s.length;const half=(n-1)>>1;let i=0,j=half+1,skipped=false;while(i<half+1 && j<n){if(s[i]===s[j]){i++;j++;}else if(!skipped){skipped=true;i++;}else return false;}return true;}function canDeleteFromRight(s){const n=s.length;const half=(n-1)>>1;let i=0,j=half,skipped=false;while(i<half && j<n){if(s[i]===s[j]){i++;j++;}else if(!skipped){skipped=true;j++;}else return false;}return true;}function isSpecial(s){const n=s.length;if(n===1) return false;if((n&1)===0) return isDouble(s);return canDeleteFromLeft(s)||canDeleteFromRight(s);}for(let t=0;t<D;++t){const s=lines[idx++]||'';out.push(isSpecial(s)?'YES':'NO');}console.log(out.join('\n'));
